veteran.reason: The bill's list of acceptable documentary proof of citizenship specifically includes a U.S. military identification card together with a military service record showing U.S. birthplace, a document option uniquely available to those with military service. This creates a trait-specific pathway for veterans that differs from the general population's registration process.

has_disability.reason: The bill's citizenship-proof requirements apply to all voters equally, but it explicitly directs states to ensure reasonable accommodations so individuals with disabilities can present documentary proof, giving them a distinct first-order interaction with the process.

student.reason: The bill's proof-of-citizenship registration requirement applies uniformly to all voter registration applicants regardless of student status.

has_student_loans.reason: This bill concerns proof of citizenship for voter registration and has no bearing on student loan borrowers.

parent_of_minor.reason: The bill concerns voter registration citizenship proof requirements and does not affect people differently based on parenting minor children.

caregiver_of_adult.reason: The bill's voter registration requirements do not vary based on whether someone cares for a dependent adult.

homeowner.reason: This voter registration citizenship bill applies identically regardless of housing status.

renter.reason: The bill's requirements do not vary by housing status.

other.reason: Voter registration proof requirements apply the same regardless of living arrangement.

citizen.reason: Citizens are the direct subject: to register to vote in federal elections they must now present documentary proof of U.S. citizenship, a firsthand new burden on every registering citizen.

permanent_resident.reason: As noncitizens, lawful permanent residents are explicit subjects of the bill's screening: they cannot register, are subject to citizenship-verification checks against databases, and face removal from rolls if registered.

other_status.reason: Visa holders, asylees, and undocumented persons are explicit subjects targeted by the bill, including verification, exclusion from registration, and referral of unlawfully registered aliens for removal proceedings.

single.reason: Never-married applicants are unlikely to face birth-certificate name mismatches, and the bill's registration rules do not otherwise turn on marital status.

married.reason: Applicants who changed their surname upon marriage may have a birth certificate that no longer matches their current name, triggering the bill's discrepancy-resolution process; this effect flows specifically from a marriage-based name change.

divorced.reason: Those who changed or reverted their name through divorce may encounter name mismatches between their birth certificate and current identification, invoking the bill's documentary-discrepancy procedures.

widowed.reason: Widowed applicants who took or retained a spouse's name may face birth-certificate name discrepancies under the proof-of-citizenship requirement, an effect tied to marriage-related name change.

under_18.reason: Voter registration for federal elections generally requires being 18, so minors are essentially unaffected by these registration proof requirements.

age_18_25.reason: This bracket includes people newly aging into voting eligibility who must register for the first time and thus directly encounter the new documentary-proof-of-citizenship requirement, an age-linked first-time event.

age_26_49.reason: The proof-of-citizenship registration requirement applies identically to all adult registrants regardless of this age bracket.

age_50_64.reason: The requirement applies uniformly to adult registrants and does not vary because of this age range.

age_65_plus.reason: The requirement applies uniformly to adult registrants and does not vary because of this age range.

under_30k.reason: The bill requires all registrants to present documentary proof of citizenship; obtaining acceptable documents (passports, certified birth certificates) carries fixed fees that impose a relatively heavier financial burden on the lowest-income households.

income_30k_60k.reason: The registration requirement applies identically regardless of income at this level.

income_60k_120k.reason: The requirement applies identically regardless of income.

income_120k_250k.reason: The requirement applies identically regardless of income.

income_250k_plus.reason: The requirement applies identically regardless of income.

education.reason: The bill governs voter registration citizenship requirements and does not touch teachers or school work.

healthcare.reason: No effect on clinical or allied health work.

manufacturing.reason: No relationship to industrial or production work.

agriculture.reason: No relationship to farming or related work.

transportation_logistics.reason: No relationship to drivers or logistics work.

public_sector_first_responders.reason: The bill imposes direct new duties on state and local election officials and federal agency employees (DHS, SSA), including verification, notification, and information-sharing obligations, and creates criminal penalties and a private right of action against election officials who register applicants without citizenship proof.

professional_office.reason: No occupation-specific effect on white-collar or office roles.

retail_service.reason: No relationship to retail or service work.

technology.reason: No occupation-specific effect on tech workers despite database provisions.

other.reason: No distinct occupational group otherwise targeted.

employee.reason: Voter registration citizenship requirements apply regardless of employment status.

self_employed_owner.reason: The bill's effect does not vary by employment status.

unemployed.reason: No effect differs based on being unemployed.

retired.reason: Registration requirements are identical regardless of retirement status.

not_in_labor_force.reason: The bill affects voter registration uniformly across labor force participation.

employer_plan.reason: This voter registration bill has no connection to health coverage source.

medicare.reason: Bill concerns voter registration citizenship requirements, unrelated to Medicare.

medicaid.reason: No relevance to Medicaid enrollment or coverage.

marketplace_individual.reason: Bill does not touch ACA or individual health plans.

va_tricare.reason: No relevance to military or veterans health coverage.

uninsured.reason: Bill is about voter registration, unrelated to insurance status.

male.reason: The documentary-proof-of-citizenship requirements apply identically to male applicants; no effect differs by being male.

female.reason: Because women disproportionately change surnames upon marriage, their current legal name may not match the name on a birth certificate, creating a documentation discrepancy under the proof-of-citizenship process. This is a first-order effect that varies with this trait due to naming conventions.

nonbinary_other.reason: The voter registration requirements do not vary by gender identity.

lgbtq.reason: This bill concerns documentary proof of citizenship for federal voter registration and does not address sexual orientation or gender identity; any effect applies identically regardless of LGBTQ+ status.

christian.reason: Voter citizenship requirements apply identically regardless of religion.

jewish.reason: No religion-specific effect from this voter registration bill.

muslim.reason: No religion-specific effect from this voter registration bill.

hindu.reason: No religion-specific effect from this voter registration bill.

buddhist.reason: No religion-specific effect from this voter registration bill.

other_faith.reason: No religion-specific effect from this voter registration bill.

none.reason: No religion-specific effect from this voter registration bill.

other_criteria: applicants seeking to register to vote in federal elections
target_groups.reason: The bill requires every applicant to present documentary proof of U.S. citizenship to register to vote in federal elections.
Non-citizens cannot register and may be identified, removed from rolls, and referred for removal-proceeding investigation.
Non-citizens are barred from registration and subject to identification, removal from voter rolls, and investigation for removal proceedings.
The bill requires states to make reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities to present documentary proof of citizenship.
